title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {{pc: Pattern Causality Analysis}},   author = {Wenbo Lyu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1},   doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.pc}, }

title: pc | Pattern Causality Analysis
text: logo layeyo Pattern Causality Analysis pc R package pattern-based causality analysis time series spatial cross-sectional data. uses symbolic pattern representations cross mapping detect directional interactions infer causal structure temporal dynamics spatial snapshots. Built high-performance C++ backend lightweight R interface, pc provides efficient flexible tools data-driven causality analysis. Refer package documentation https://stscl.github.io/pc/ detailed information.

title: pc | Pattern Causality Analysis
text: Install CRAN : Install binary version R-universe : Install source code GitHub :
code: install.packages("pc", dependencies = TRUE) install.packages("pc",                  repos = c("https://stscl.r-universe.dev",                            "https://cloud.r-project.org"),                  dependencies = TRUE) if (!requireNamespace("pak")) {     install.packages("pak") } pak::pak("stscl/pc", dependencies = TRUE)

what: Optimal Parameter Search for Pattern Causality — ops
title: Optimal Parameter Search for Pattern Causality — ops
Optimal Parameter Search for Pattern Causality — ops
code: # S4 method for class 'data.frame' ops(   data,   target,   source,   E = 3:5,   k = E,   tau = 1,   style = 1,   lib = NULL,   pred = NULL,   maximize = c("positive", "negative", "dark"),   dist.metric = c("euclidean", "manhattan", "maximum"),   zero.tolerance = max(k),   relative = TRUE,   weighted = TRUE,   threads = length(E),   higher.parallel = TRUE,   h = 0 )  # S4 method for class 'sf' ops(   data,   target,   source,   E = 3:5,   k = E + 1,   tau = 1,   style = 1,   lib = NULL,   pred = NULL,   maximize = c("positive", "negative", "dark"),   dist.metric = c("euclidean", "manhattan", "maximum"),   zero.tolerance = max(k),   relative = TRUE,   weighted = TRUE,   threads = length(E),   higher.parallel = TRUE,   nb = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'SpatRaster' ops(   data,   target,   source,   E = 3:5,   k = E + 1,   tau = 1,   style = 1,   lib = NULL,   pred = NULL,   maximize = c("positive", "negative", "dark"),   dist.metric = c("euclidean", "manhattan", "maximum"),   zero.tolerance = max(k),   relative = TRUE,   weighted = TRUE,   threads = length(E),   higher.parallel = TRUE )

title: Optimal Parameter Search for Pattern Causality — ops
text: data Observation data. target Integer column indice target variable. source Integer column indice source variable. E (optional) Embedding dimensions. k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used projection. tau (optional) Step lag. style (optional) Embedding style (0 includes current state, 1 excludes ). lib (optional) Libraries indices. pred (optional) Predictions indices. maximize (optional) Causality metric maximize: one "positive", "negative", "dark". dist.metric (optional) Distance measure used. zero.tolerance (optional) Maximum number zeros tolerated signature space. relative (optional) Whether calculate relative changes embedding. weighted (optional) Whether weight causal strength. threads (optional) Number threads used. higher.parallel (optional) Whether use higher level parallelism. h (optional) Prediction horizon. nb (optional) Neighbours list.

title: Optimal Parameter Search for Pattern Causality — ops
text: list. param selected optimal parameter combination. xmap data.frame containing cross-mapping performance across parameter settings.

title: Optimal Parameter Search for Pattern Causality — ops
code: columbus = sf::read_sf(system.file("case/columbus.gpkg", package="spEDM")) pc::ops(columbus, 1, 3, E = 5:10, maximize = "negative", threads = 1) #> The suggested E, k, tau is 5, 9 and 1

title: Pattern Causality — pc
code: # S4 method for class 'data.frame' pc(   data,   target,   source,   libsizes = NULL,   E = 3,   k = E,   tau = 1,   style = 1,   lib = NULL,   pred = NULL,   boot = 99,   random = TRUE,   seed = 42L,   dist.metric = c("euclidean", "manhattan", "maximum"),   zero.tolerance = max(k),   relative = TRUE,   weighted = TRUE,   threads = length(libsizes),   higher.parallel = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   h = 0 )  # S4 method for class 'sf' pc(   data,   target,   source,   libsizes = NULL,   E = 3,   k = E + 1,   tau = 1,   style = 1,   lib = NULL,   pred = NULL,   boot = 99,   random = TRUE,   seed = 42L,   dist.metric = c("euclidean", "manhattan", "maximum"),   zero.tolerance = max(k),   relative = TRUE,   weighted = TRUE,   threads = length(libsizes),   higher.parallel = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   nb = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'SpatRaster' pc(   data,   target,   source,   libsizes = NULL,   E = 3,   k = E + 1,   tau = 1,   style = 1,   lib = NULL,   pred = NULL,   boot = 99,   random = TRUE,   seed = 42L,   dist.metric = c("euclidean", "manhattan", "maximum"),   zero.tolerance = max(k),   relative = TRUE,   weighted = TRUE,   threads = length(libsizes),   higher.parallel = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Pattern Causality — pc
text: data Observation data. target Integer column indice target variable. source Integer column indice source variable. libsizes (optional) Number observations used. E (optional) Embedding dimensions. k (optional) Number nearest neighbors used projection. tau (optional) Step lag. style (optional) Embedding style (0 includes current state, 1 excludes ). lib (optional) Libraries indices. pred (optional) Predictions indices. boot (optional) Number bootstraps perform. random (optional) Whether use random sampling. seed (optional) Random seed. dist.metric (optional) Distance measure used. zero.tolerance (optional) Maximum number zeros tolerated signature space. relative (optional) Whether calculate relative changes embedding. weighted (optional) Whether weight causal strength. threads (optional) Number threads used. higher.parallel (optional) Whether use higher level parallelism. verbose (optional) Whether show progress bar. h (optional) Prediction horizon. nb (optional) Neighbours list.

title: Pattern Causality — pc
text: list (libsizes NULL) data.frame. libsizes NULL, list two components returned: causality data.frame containing per-sample causality results. summary data.frame summarizing overall causality metrics.

title: Pattern Causality — pc
text: Stavroglou, S.K., Pantelous, .., Stanley, H.E., Zuev, K.M., 2020. Unveiling causal interactions complex systems. Proceedings National Academy Sciences 117, 7599–7605.

title: Pattern Causality — pc
code: columbus = sf::read_sf(system.file("case/columbus.gpkg", package="spEDM")) pc::pc(columbus, 1, 3, E = 5, k = 10, threads = 1) #>       type  strength #> 1 positive       NaN #> 2 negative 0.6765119 #> 3     dark 0.6273476
